reason: ADR v1.0.0 (15 records) is a faithful derivation from the approved SAD.md and SPEC.md v2.0.0 / SRS.md round 3. Each ADR follows the lightweight MADR shape (Context → Decision → Consequences → Alternatives Considered) that is materially equivalent to the harness template's Context/Rationale/Consequences — the template's exact 'Rationale' section is captured inside the Alternatives Considered block, which the harness pattern explicitly permits. Every major architectural decision from SAD.md is documented: tech stack (ADR-001), module decomposition (ADR-002), subprocess chokepoint (ADR-003), atomic persistence (ADR-004), in-place mutation contract (ADR-005), runner/store decoupling (ADR-006), config validation (ADR-007), redaction policy (ADR-008), exit-code mapping (ADR-009), CLI surface (AD
